prd_text: REQ-PAY-001: Instant settlement SHALL be available exclusively to merchants on the Premium subscription tier. Standard and Basic tier merchants will continue to use T+1 batch settlement. The premium tier instant settlement window is defined as within 90 seconds of transaction approval.
ticket_text:
TICKET PAY-1042
Title: Roll out instant settlement to all merchant tiers
Status: Done
Assignee: Jordan L.
Description: Per Q3 planning session and PM confirmation in Slack (Sept 12), instant settlement is now being expanded to ALL tiers—Basic, Standard, and Premium. Implemented by removing the tier guard in settlement_service.py. No PRD update made.
discussion_snippet:
Slack #payments-eng Sept 12
Alex: Quick heads-up—instant settlement is going to all tiers now, not just premium. Jordan can you handle that?
Jordan: On it. Will remove the premium check. PRD update needed?
Alex: We'll update it later, just ship it.
api_spec_excerpt:
POST /v3/payments
Field settlement_tier: enum [instant, standard]
Description: 'All merchant tiers are eligible for instant settlement as of v3.0'
code_diff_summary: settlement_service.py: Removed `if merchant.tier == 'premium'` guard from instant_settle(). All merchants now routed to instant path by default. No unit tests added for Basic/Standard tier instant settlement path.
test_case_text:
TC-PAY-004: Instant settlement — Premium tier: PASS
TC-PAY-005: Instant settlement — Basic tier: NOT WRITTEN
TC-PAY-006: Instant settlement — Standard tier: NOT WRITTEN
release_note: Payments v3.0: Instant settlement now available for Premium merchants.
label: contradictory
rationale: PRD restricts instant settlement to Premium only. Ticket, code, and API spec expand it to all tiers. Release note still says Premium only. Four artifacts contradict each other on a core business rule.

prd_text: REQ-PAY-003: The fraud detection model SHALL achieve a minimum precision of 99.2% on the monthly holdout validation set. This threshold is required for PCI-DSS Level 1 compliance. Any model version failing this threshold SHALL NOT be deployed to production.
ticket_text:
TICKET PAY-1089
Title: Deploy fraud model v4.2 to production
Status: Done
Assignee: ML Team
Notes: Model v4.2 achieves 98.8% precision on holdout. Discussed lowering threshold informally—Alex approved shipping at 98.8% in Slack. No formal exception documented.
discussion_snippet:
Slack #ml-team Sept 14
Alex: Let's just ship at 98.8%, we'll improve in Q4.
Morgan: Compliance requires 99% minimum for PCI-DSS Level 1. We can't go below that.
Alex: Ship it quietly, don't put the number in release notes.
Riley: That's a governance violation. This needs to be escalated.
code_diff_summary: fraud_model_config.py: model_version set to '4.2'. Actual precision in model registry: 0.988. API response hardcoded to return 0.992 for compliance display purposes.
test_case_text:
TC-FRAUD-001: Model precision validation
Expected: >= 99.2%
Actual: 98.8%
Status: FAIL
release_note: Payments v3.0: Real-time fraud detection with 99.2% precision.
label: release_mismatch
rationale: Release note states 99.2% precision. Test confirms actual precision is 98.8%. API response is hardcoded to return the incorrect figure. Published release notes contain a factually false safety metric.

prd_text: REQ-AUTH-004: Session tokens SHALL expire after 8 hours of inactivity. This is a hard security requirement for SOC 2 Type II compliance. Tenant administrators SHALL NOT be able to override this limit.
ticket_text:
TICKET AUTH-301
Title: Make session timeout configurable per tenant
Status: Done
Assignee: Jordan L.
Description: Enterprise customers requested longer sessions. Implemented configurable timeout: default 8h, tenants can set 1h to 168h (7 days). Shipped to main without PRD or security doc update.
discussion_snippet:
Teams #auth-redesign Sept 10
Alex: Let's make it 24h default, enterprise can go up to 7 days.
Morgan: Legal says anything over 24h needs a security exception sign-off.
Jordan: Already shipped 24h default with 168h max tenant config. Docs TBD.
api_spec_excerpt:
PATCH /v1/tenant/settings
Field session_timeout_hours: integer (1-168)
Description: 'Configure session expiry per tenant. Default: 24.'
code_diff_summary: session_manager.py: Default timeout changed from 8h to 24h. Added tenant_session_timeout config field with range 1-168. No documentation updated. No security review.
test_case_text:
TC-AUTH-004: Session expiry at 8h: SKIPPED (behavior changed)
TC-AUTH-011: Session expiry at 24h default: PASS
TC-AUTH-012: Tenant config 168h max: PASS
release_note: Auth Service v2.0: Enterprise-grade SSO and MFA. Session management improvements.
label: undocumented_change
rationale: PRD mandates 8h non-configurable timeout. Code ships 24h default with 168h tenant-configurable max. PRD, security docs, and release notes not updated. Violates SOC 2 control without documented exception.

prd_text: REQ-AUTH-002: Multi-factor authentication SHALL be enforced for all users assigned the admin role. MFA cannot be disabled or bypassed for admin accounts. This control is required for SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 compliance.
ticket_text:
TICKET AUTH-204
Title: Add MFA bypass for CI/CD service accounts
Status: Done
Assignee: Jordan L.
Description: Service accounts used in pipelines cannot do MFA. Added skip_mfa boolean flag to User model. When true, MFA is bypassed regardless of role. Verbal approval from Taylor (admin).
discussion_snippet:
Teams #infra-ops
Jordan: CI/CD pipelines break with MFA enforcement. Adding a bypass flag.
Taylor: Fine, approved.
Morgan: This should go through the security exception process.
Jordan: Taylor approved it, shipping now.
api_spec_excerpt:
POST /v1/users
Field skip_mfa: boolean (optional)
Description: 'If true, MFA enforcement is skipped for this account.'
code_diff_summary: auth_middleware.py: Added check `if user.skip_mfa: return next()` before MFA validation. This applies to ALL roles including admin. No tests for admin + skip_mfa combination. No audit log entry when flag is used.
test_case_text:
TC-AUTH-002: MFA enforced for admin role: PASS (skip_mfa=false)
TC-AUTH-013: Admin + skip_mfa=true: NOT WRITTEN
release_note: Auth Service v2.0: Robust MFA enforcement for enterprise accounts.
label: undocumented_change
rationale: A security control (mandatory admin MFA) was bypassed without formal exception, audit trail, or documentation. The release note falsely implies MFA is robustly enforced. SOC 2 audit exposure.

prd_text: REQ-NOTIF-005: The notification delivery system SHALL retry failed deliveries up to 3 times using exponential backoff with a base delay of 30 seconds. After 3 failures, the notification SHALL be moved to a dead-letter queue and an alert SHALL be raised to the on-call engineer.
ticket_text:
TICKET NOTIF-88
Title: Implement retry logic for failed notifications
Status: Done
Assignee: Sam R.
Description: Implemented retry with exponential backoff. Max retries: 3, base delay 30s. Dead-letter queue implemented. On-call alert via PagerDuty on 3rd failure.
discussion_snippet:
Teams #notifications-dev
Sam: Retry logic shipped. 3 retries, 30s base, DLQ on failure.
Morgan: Does the DLQ alert go to on-call or just log?
Sam: PagerDuty alert on 3rd failure as per PRD.
api_spec_excerpt:
GET /v1/notifications/{id}/status
Response: { status: 'delivered'|'failed'|'retrying'|'dead_lettered', retry_count: integer, next_retry_at: datetime }
code_diff_summary: notification_service.py: Implemented retry queue with max_retries=3, base_delay=30s, exponential factor=2. DLQ implemented. PagerDuty alert fired on final failure. Unit tests for all retry paths written.
test_case_text:
TC-NOTIF-005a: Retry on transient failure: PASS
TC-NOTIF-005b: Exponential backoff timing: PASS
TC-NOTIF-005c: DLQ after 3 failures: PASS
TC-NOTIF-005d: PagerDuty alert on DLQ: PASS
release_note: Notification Engine v1.0: Reliable delivery with 3-retry exponential backoff and dead-letter queue alerting.
label: aligned
rationale: PRD requirement, ticket, code implementation, tests, and release note are all fully consistent. Feature is correctly implemented and documented.

prd_text: REQ-NOTIF-007: The notification system SHALL support bulk send campaigns for up to 100,000 recipients per campaign. Bulk sends SHALL be processed asynchronously and SHALL complete within 10 minutes for a 100k-recipient campaign.
ticket_text:
TICKET NOTIF-102
Title: Implement bulk notification send
Status: Done
Assignee: Sam R.
Description: Bulk send implemented using async worker queue. Tested up to 100k recipients. Completion time: ~4 minutes in staging.
discussion_snippet:
Teams #notifications-dev
Sam: Bulk send is live. 100k recipients in about 4 minutes.
Morgan: Does it handle deduplication?
Sam: Yes, duplicate recipient IDs are silently dropped.
code_diff_summary: bulk_sender.py: Async queue-based bulk send. Max 100k recipients. Deduplication applied. Load test: 100k in 3m 42s. Tests: full suite passing.
test_case_text:
TC-NOTIF-007a: 100k recipient bulk send: PASS (3m 42s)
TC-NOTIF-007b: Duplicate recipient deduplication: PASS
TC-NOTIF-007c: Schedule future send: PASS
release_note: Notification Engine v1.0: Bulk campaign send for up to 100,000 recipients with async processing.
label: aligned
rationale: All artifacts are consistent. PRD, ticket, implementation, tests, and release note agree on the feature behavior and performance targets.

prd_text: REQ-SEARCH-003: Search results SHALL be ranked using a hybrid scoring model combining BM25 keyword relevance and semantic embedding similarity. The weighting SHALL be configurable with a default of 60% semantic / 40% BM25. Results SHALL include a relevance score between 0 and 1.
ticket_text:
TICKET SEARCH-55
Title: Implement hybrid search ranking
Status: In Progress
Assignee: Unassigned
Description: Need to implement hybrid BM25 + semantic ranking. Owner unclear—ML team or Search team? No decision recorded.
discussion_snippet:
Teams #search-platform
Alex: Who owns the hybrid ranking implementation?
ML Team Lead: We own the embedding model but not the BM25 integration.
Search Lead: We own BM25 but not the embedding pipeline.
Alex: Let's revisit in next sprint.
code_diff_summary: search_service.py: BM25 ranking implemented. Semantic scoring: NOT implemented. Hybrid weighting: NOT implemented. relevance_score currently returns raw BM25 score normalized 0-1.
test_case_text:
TC-SEARCH-003: Hybrid ranking accuracy test: NOT WRITTEN
TC-SEARCH-004: BM25 baseline ranking: PASS
TC-SEARCH-005: Semantic reranking: NOT WRITTEN
release_note: Search v2.0: Improved search relevance with hybrid ranking.
label: unclear_owner
rationale: The hybrid ranking feature has no assigned owner. ML team and Search team both disclaim ownership. Implementation is partial (BM25 only, no semantic). Tests for hybrid behavior not written. Release note implies full hybrid is live.

prd_text: REQ-EXPORT-004: The data export API SHALL enforce rate limiting of 10 export requests per user per hour. Requests exceeding this limit SHALL receive HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header. Rate limit counters SHALL reset at the top of each UTC hour.
ticket_text:
TICKET EXPORT-33
Title: Add rate limiting to export API
Status: Done
Assignee: Lee P.
Description: Rate limiting added using Redis sliding window. Limit: 10 requests per user per hour. Returns 429 with Retry-After on breach.
discussion_snippet:
Teams #data-platform
Lee: Rate limiting shipped. Redis sliding window, 10/hour.
Alex: Sliding window or fixed window? PRD says reset at top of hour which sounds fixed.
Lee: I used sliding window—it's more accurate. Same practical result.
code_diff_summary: export_limiter.py: Redis sliding window counter. 10 requests per 3600s per user_id. Retry-After header set to seconds until window clears. Tests passing for limit enforcement.
test_case_text:
TC-EXPORT-004a: 10 requests allowed: PASS
TC-EXPORT-004b: 11th request returns 429: PASS
TC-EXPORT-004c: Retry-After header present: PASS
TC-EXPORT-004d: Fixed-window reset at top of hour: NOT TESTED (sliding window used instead)
release_note: Data Export v1.1: Rate limiting enforced — 10 exports per hour per user.
label: ambiguous
rationale: PRD specifies fixed-window reset at top of UTC hour. Implementation uses sliding window. Both are 'rate limiting' but have different behavior near window boundaries. The difference is undiscussed in the ticket and untested. Ambiguity about which is correct.

prd_text: REQ-BILL-007: When a customer downgrades their subscription tier mid-billing-cycle, they SHALL receive a prorated credit for the remaining days on the higher tier. Credits SHALL be applied to the next invoice automatically.
ticket_text:
TICKET BILL-201
Title: Implement prorated refund on downgrade
Status: Done
Assignee: Chris T.
Description: Prorated credit calculation implemented. Credit applied to next invoice. Tested with multiple downgrade scenarios.
discussion_snippet:
Teams #billing-dev
Chris: Prorate logic is live. Tested basic and premium to standard downgrades.
Alex: What about annual plan downgrades?
Chris: Good catch—annual plans aren't handled yet. Opened BILL-209 as a follow-up.
code_diff_summary: billing_service.py: Prorated credit for monthly plan downgrades. Annual plans: credit calculation returns 0 due to unimplemented annual prorate logic. BILL-209 created.
test_case_text:
TC-BILL-007a: Monthly plan downgrade prorate: PASS
TC-BILL-007b: Annual plan downgrade prorate: FAIL (returns 0 credit)
release_note: Billing v2.1: Prorated credits applied automatically on subscription downgrades.
label: missing_acceptance_criteria
rationale: PRD describes prorate for all downgrade scenarios but does not distinguish monthly vs annual. Acceptance criteria omit the annual plan case. The partial implementation (monthly only) passes existing tests but fails for annual plans, exposing a gap in criteria.

prd_text: REQ-ONBOARD-001: All new user accounts SHALL require email verification before accessing the product. An email verification link SHALL be sent on registration and SHALL expire after 24 hours. Unverified accounts SHALL have read-only access.
ticket_text:
TICKET ONBOARD-15
Title: Implement email verification flow
Status: Done
Assignee: Dana W.
Description: Email verification implemented. Link expiry: 24 hours. Unverified users are blocked entirely (no read-only access, full block).
discussion_snippet:
Teams #onboarding
Dana: Email verify is live. Unverified users are fully blocked.
Alex: PRD says read-only, not full block. Did we change this?
Dana: Full block is better UX-wise—prevents confusion. No ticket for the change.
api_spec_excerpt:
GET /v1/me
If unverified: HTTP 403 { error: 'email_not_verified', message: 'Please verify your email to access this resource.' }
code_diff_summary: auth_middleware.py: Unverified users receive HTTP 403 on all endpoints. PRD specified read-only access for unverified users but full block was implemented as an undocumented product decision.
test_case_text:
TC-ONBOARD-001a: Verified user — full access: PASS
TC-ONBOARD-001b: Unverified user — read-only access: FAIL (returns 403 instead)
TC-ONBOARD-001c: Verification link expiry at 24h: PASS
release_note: Onboarding v1.0: Email verification required for all new accounts.
label: contradictory
rationale: PRD specifies read-only access for unverified users. Code and API spec implement full block (403). Test TC-ONBOARD-001b fails. An undocumented product decision changed the behavior without a PRD update.

prd_text: REQ-SEARCH-007: The search API SHALL provide query autocomplete suggestions based on historical popular searches and the current user's search history. Suggestions SHALL be returned within 50ms at p99. A maximum of 8 suggestions SHALL be returned per query.
ticket_text:
TICKET SEARCH-71
Title: Add autocomplete to search
Status: Discussed, not started
Assignee: (none)
Description: Autocomplete was scoped in the Q3 roadmap discussion. No implementation started. Deprioritized due to infrastructure work but never formally removed from scope.
discussion_snippet:
Teams #search-platform Q3 planning
Alex: Autocomplete is a Q3 feature, let's make sure it makes the cut.
Search Lead: It's on the list but we haven't started. Might slip to Q4.
Alex: Keep it in scope for now.
api_spec_excerpt:
GET /v1/search/autocomplete?q={prefix}
Status: NOT IMPLEMENTED — endpoint returns 501
code_diff_summary: search_api.py: /v1/search/autocomplete returns HTTP 501 Not Implemented. No autocomplete logic written. Feature was discussed in planning but never assigned or started.
test_case_text: TC-SEARCH-007: Autocomplete returns 8 suggestions within 50ms: NOT WRITTEN
release_note: Search v2.0: Enhanced search experience with improved relevance and performance.
label: discussed_not_implemented
rationale: Feature was committed to in PRD and roadmap discussions but never implemented. API returns 501. No tests written. Release note omits the feature but product team believes it shipped.

prd_text: REQ-EXPORT-001: Users SHALL be able to select which columns to include in CSV data exports. The export API SHALL accept a column_filter parameter. If omitted, all columns SHALL be exported. Column selection SHALL be persisted per user as a default preference.
ticket_text:
TICKET EXPORT-11
Title: Implement CSV column selection for exports
Status: Done
Assignee: Lee P.
Description: column_filter parameter implemented. Filters columns in output. Preference persistence: not implemented (EXPORT-22 opened as follow-up).
discussion_snippet:
Teams #data-platform
Lee: Column selection is live. Persistence is in EXPORT-22.
Alex: When does EXPORT-22 ship?
Lee: Q4. It's a nice-to-have.
Alex: PRD says SHALL persist. That's a requirement, not nice-to-have.
api_spec_excerpt:
GET /v1/export/data?column_filter=id,name,email
Response: CSV with selected columns.
Note: column_filter persistence not yet implemented.
code_diff_summary: export_service.py: column_filter parsing and CSV generation implemented. UserPreferences.default_column_filter: NOT implemented. EXPORT-22 created as follow-up.
test_case_text:
TC-EXPORT-001a: Column filter applied to output: PASS
TC-EXPORT-001b: Default all columns when filter omitted: PASS
TC-EXPORT-001c: Column preference persisted per user: NOT WRITTEN
release_note: Data Export v1.0: Flexible CSV exports with column selection.
label: missing_test
rationale: PRD requires preference persistence (SHALL). Implementation defers it to a follow-up ticket. No test written for the persistence requirement. The release note implies full feature delivery without acknowledging the gap.
